what_it_does: This file is a self-contained HTTP service adapter that loads the Coqui XTTS-v2 multilingual text-to-speech model on demand and exposes two engine operations: synthesize speech from text using a real speaker reference clip, and report the configured voice. It runs as a standalone service on port 4127 and is intended only as a non-commercial trial.
observations: Top-of-file docstring frames XTTS-v2 as a trial/non-commercial voice due to the Coqui Public Model License.
Requires the environment variable COQUI_TOS_AGREED=1 and sets it non-interactively via os.environ.setdefault.
Uses environment variables for DEVICE, MODEL, VOICE_REF, and LANG with sensible defaults.
Implements lazy singleton loading of the TTS model in _engine().
synth() enforces that a real speaker_wav file exists and refuses to fabricate one.
Passes the speed argument through to the underlying tts() call.
Returns WAV bytes at 24000 Hz via wav_bytes_from_array imported from voice.engines._service.
voices() simply returns the configured reference path as the only available voice.
__main__ starts the HTTP service on a configurable port and warms the engine.
standouts: Explicit license gate and trial-only framing embedded in both code and docstring.
Non-interactive auto-acceptance of the Coqui TOS via os.environ.setdefault.
Zero-shot voice cloning contingent on an external, real speaker reference clip.
conventions: Follows the engine service pattern imported from voice.engines._service.
Uses lazy initialization for a heavy ML model.
concerns: Auto-setting COQUI_TOS_AGREED=1 may silently accept legal terms in production deployments.
No validation of the speed parameter range before passing it to the model.
If COMPANY_VOICE_REF is unset, the engine warms successfully but synth() fails at request time.
Global mutable _tts state with no thread-safety comments.
notes: A license-gated, trial-only XTTS-v2 voice engine wrapper that insists on a real speaker clip and exposes synthesis through a shared service interface.
questions: What is the exact HTTP API contract provided by voice.engines._service?
Where is the 'refined-Australian' reference clip stored and how is it produced?
Does the installed coqui-tts version actually support a speed argument in tts()?
How are concurrent requests to the global _tts instance handled?
What happens if the configured CUDA device is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — an XTTS-v2 HTTP service on port 4127 for trial evaluation — matches what the code does.
apparent_themes: Minimal adapter/wrapper over a third-party TTS library
intention_signals: The file is trying to give the project a quick way to evaluate high-realism cloned speech while keeping the non-commercial XTTS-v2 model legally and operationally separated from any commercial voice path.
novelty: The unusual element is the prominent, in-code legal/trial disclaimer combined with non-interactive TOS acceptance and a hard refusal to synthesize without a real speaker reference.
contribution: Provides a self-contained XTTS-v2 inference adapter that enforces a real speaker reference, wires speed through the synthesis contract, and exposes the standard engine service functions (synth, voices, warm) while making its non-commercial license scope explicit.
summary_for_embedding: voice/engines/xtts.py is a license-gated, trial-only HTTP service adapter for Coqui XTTS-v2 that lazily loads the model, requires a real speaker reference clip, and returns 24 kHz WAV bytes via a shared engine service interface.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to let the project evaluate realistic zero-shot voice cloning with XTTS-v2 while keeping the non-commercial model legally isolated from production use.

symbols.does: Lazily loads the Coqui XTTS-v2 TTS model onto the configured device and returns the singleton instance.
symbols.description: _engine() initializes the global _tts object on first call, sets COQUI_TOS_AGREED=1 by default, imports TTS.api.TTS, instantiates the configured model, moves it to DEVICE, and caches it for reuse.

symbols.does: Synthesizes speech for the given text using a real speaker reference clip and returns WAV bytes.
symbols.description: synth() validates that a speaker_wav path is provided and exists, calls the loaded XTTS model with text, speaker_wav, language, and speed, then converts the returned float waveform into WAV bytes at 24000 Hz.

symbols.does: Reports the single configured voice option, which is the path to the speaker reference clip.
symbols.description: voices() returns a tuple containing a list with the configured VOICE_REF path and a display string; if no reference is configured, the placeholder '(set COMPANY_VOICE_REF)' is returned.
